title: Asian Stocks to Track US Drop on Inflation Fears: Markets Wrap
summary: Asian stocks were set to follow Wall Street lower after mounting inflation concerns pushed the yield on the longest-dated US Treasury bond to its highest level since 2007.

title: Oil Holds Decline as Traders Weigh Trump’s Latest Iran Threats
summary: Oil held a modest decline, as traders shrugged off President Donald Trump’s newest threat to resume strikes on Iran, a pledge the US leader has made repeatedly before backing off since a truce was agreed to in early April.

title: Building Fan Community Key to WNBA Success, Says Valkyries President Jess Smith
summary: Jess Smith, president of the Golden State Valkyries, said that building community and connection in a deeper way and then building a business around that foundation has been key to her teams success in their first season. Smith said that the Valkyries, a team that was recently rated at a $1 billion valuation, are serious about upholding 'golden state culture' and investing in the fan experience to attract a dedicated fanbase and grow the franchise as the WNBA enters its 30th season.  (Source: Bl

title: New HSBC Report Reveals Gap in Addressing Women's Financial Goals
summary:
Racquel Oden, HSBC head of international wealth and private banking, said that while women have financial literacy, many feel that they don't have the fluency to make the right decisions about their finances by themselves. 
Oden, who joined the show alongside Bloomberg senior editor Claire Obusan, broke down a new report from HSBC that revealed women feel that traditional financial advice doesn't address the complexity of their financial goals.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Heat Strains US Power Grids That Need Off-Season Repairs
summary: A blast of late-spring heat is straining electric grids in the eastern US as power-plant owners race to repair equipment ahead of rising demand and hotter-than-normal weather expected this summer.
